title: Discrete-Time Remaining-Laps Survival Instead Of Plain Next-Lap Classification
summary: Official survival docs and recent survival literature both fit this label geometry: each row has a positive time-to-event, and many rows are naturally right-censored by race finish or no later pit.
rationale: Official survival docs and recent survival literature both fit this label geometry: each row has a positive time-to-event, and many rows are naturally right-censored by race finish or no later pit. This reframes the problem as learning the full pit-window shape, then extracting the 1-lap hazard, rather than asking a standalone binary question at each lap.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race row, compute `laps_to_next_pit`; if no future pit exists, right-censor at remaining race length. Train either XGBoost `survival:aft` or a discrete-time hazard model on Kaplan-Meier-quantile bins, then convert the predicted survival curve into `P(pit on next lap | not yet pitted)` and blend that probability with the current classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve early/late-stint generalization and reduce noisy threshold behavior around pit windows, with the biggest upside on rows far from the immediate cliff.
risk: Label construction and hazard-to-probability conversion are easy to get wrong; AFT may underperform if the true hazard is highly non-proportional across compounds/regimes.
